title: NEO-LEDGER
description: A decentralized financial architecture reimagined for the brutalist web. Neo-Ledger handles multi-chain asset complexity with raw speed and zero clutter.
problem: Legacy financial platforms are trapped in 'safe' designs that compromise performance and architectural clarity.

problems.detail: Legacy systems use bloated frameworks, causing excessive bundle sizes and rendering bottlenecks on low-end devices.
Inefficient state handling leads to race conditions during critical financial transactions.
Periodic polling creates a significant visual lag during high market volatility.

solution: We enforced a strict Neobrutalist system prioritizing pure data hierarchy and uncompromising performance.

solutions.detail: Rebuilt using Next.js Server Components to drastically reduce client-side overhead.
Implemented xState for predictive transaction management, ensuring data integrity at every step.
Migrated to WebSockets for instant data updates (<100ms) without compromising browser resources.

blocks.heading: The Core Philosophy
blocks.body: In the NEO-LEDGER project, we stripped away every non-essential visual element. Every component must pass the 'Raw Logic' test: if a border doesn't define structure, it is removed.

blocks.text: Brutalism in web design isn't about being ugly; it's about being unapologetically functional.

blocks.heading: Technical Architecture
blocks.body: The system is built for real-time data synchronization across multiple blockchain protocols. We utilized a purely event-driven architecture.

blocks.heading: Key Performance Indicators:
items: Sub-100ms latency across all data visualizers.
Zero dependencies on bloated third-party UI libraries.

blocks.caption: Figure 1.0: Technical visualization of decentralized node clusters.

technical.frontend: Architected with Next.js 16 for peak server-side rendering performance.
technical.realtime: Leveraging WebSockets and GraphQL for instant data streams.
technical.security: End-to-end encrypted session management and hardware layer integration.

title: BRUTALIST UI KIT
description: A collection of 50+ neobrutalist components for Figma and Webflow, designed for high-impact landing pages.
problem: Modern UI kits often focus on 'safe' aesthetics that fail to capture a brand's raw energy.

problems.detail: Most UI kits today look uniform, lacking the strong visual character needed to stand out in the market.
Soft shadows and pastel colors make websites look generic to progressive tech audiences.

solution: We broke conventional rules by introducing heavy 8px solid borders and dominant typography.

solutions.detail: Provides a very firm visual structure so that information hierarchy becomes crystal clear.
Uses maximum color saturation to grab audience attention instantly and boldly.

blocks.heading: Visual Identity
blocks.body: We believe that design should have an opinion. Brutalist UI Kit is not for everyone; it is a tool for those who dare to be bold and defy the tide of boring minimalism.

technical.frontend: Pure CSS with zero additional abstraction layers for peak performance.
